name: PULMONARY VEIN VARIX
definition: abnormal tortuosity + focal dilatation of pulmonary vein just before entrance into left atrium

demographics.Etiology: congenital / associated with chronic pulmonary venous hypertension or mitral valve
demographics.Location: medial ⅓ of either lung below hila close to left atrium

dominant_finding: well-defined lobulated round / oval mass without Ca2+
distribution: medial ⅓ of either lung below hila close to left atrium
discriminating_features: well-defined lobulated round / oval mass without Ca2+
change in size during Valsalva / Müller maneuver
opacification at same time as LA (on CECT)

general: well-defined lobulated round / oval mass without Ca2+
change in size during Valsalva / Müller maneuver
opacification at same time as LA (on CECT)
